intro.uz: Identity — oʻzingiz haqingizda ishonadigan narsalaringiz, yaʼni “men kimman” degan global eʼtiqodlar. AJ Hoge ularni oʻzgartirib boʻlishini oʻrgatadi: bugungi “men” — oʻtmishdagi “men”dan boshqa. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi shu yoʻlga mos: oʻtgan zamon hikoyasi va ketma-ketlik (then / after that / finally) hamda oʻzgarishni ifodalash (get / become + sifat).
intro.en: Identity is what you believe about yourself — the global beliefs that answer “who am I?”. AJ Hoge teaches that these can be changed: the person you are today is not the person you were. This week's two grammar topics fit that journey: past simple narrative and sequencing (then / after that / finally), and expressing change (get / become + adjective).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻz oʻtmishingizdagi bir voqeani toʻgʻri oʻtgan zamon va ketma-ketlik soʻzlari bilan hikoya qilib bera olasiz, hamda oʻzingiz qanday oʻzgarganingizni (I became…, my English is getting…) ayta olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can tell a story from your past using correct past simple and sequencing words, and describe how you have changed (I became…, my English is getting…).

grammar.titleUz: Oʻtgan zamon hikoyasi va ketma-ketlik (then / after that / finally; notoʻgʻri feʼllar)
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — tell a story about something that happened in the past
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can narrate past events in sequence using past simple
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida oʻtgan zamon shaxsga qarab oʻzgaradi (bordim, bording, bordi), ingliz tilida esa past simple hamma shaxs uchun bitta shakl: I/you/he/they went. Faqat “be” feʼli istisno (I was / they were). Qiyini — koʻp ishlatiladigan feʼllar notoʻgʻri: become → became, think → thought, feel → felt, have → had.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ He becomed a taxi driver. He thinked it was exciting. ✅ He became a taxi driver. He thought it was exciting. Notoʻgʻri feʼllarga -ed qoʻshilmaydi — ularning maxsus oʻtgan shakli bor (became, thought, felt, got, went).

examples.en: Ken was a bored accountant, so he quit his job.
examples.uz: Ken zerikkan buxgalter edi, shuning uchun ishini tashladi.

examples.en: First he became a taxi driver, then he changed his beliefs.
examples.uz: Avval u taksichi boʻldi, keyin eʼtiqodlarini oʻzgartirdi.

examples.en: Finally, he became a vampire killer and felt happy.
examples.uz: Nihoyat, u vampir ovchisi boʻldi va oʻzini baxtli his qildi.

exercises.prompt: He quit his job and ___ a taxi driver.
exercises.hintUz: become notoʻgʻri feʼl: oʻtgan shakli — became (becomed emas)

exercises.prompt: ___ he changed his beliefs and got a new identity.
exercises.hintUz: ketma-ketlik: after that = shundan soʻng (jumla boshida)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost hikoya ayting (ovoz chiqarib): oʻtmishingizdagi uchta ish, ketma-ket — “First I … Then I … Finally I …”.
exercises.hintUz: past simple + first/then/finally; notoʻgʻri feʼllarga eʼtibor bering

grammar.titleUz: Oʻzgarishni ifodalash: get / become / turn + sifat
grammar.titleEn: Expressing change — get / become / turn + adjective
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — say how your English (or your life) has changed
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can describe a change of state (I got tired; I became confident)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida oʻzgarishni bitta feʼl bilan aytamiz: -lashmoq / -lashib bormoq / boʻlmoq (kuchaydi, zerikdi, sovidi). Ingliz tili esa “feʼl + sifat” juftligini talab qiladi: became strong, got bored, turned cold. get — ogʻzaki, jonli nutqda; become — biroz rasmiy; turn — rang/ob-havo yoki keskin oʻzgarish uchun.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ He became to a taxi driver. My English becomes good. ✅ He became a taxi driver. My English is getting better. become dan keyin “to” qoʻyilmaydi; davom etayotgan oʻzgarish uchun esa get (is getting) tabiiyroq eshitiladi.

examples.en: I got bored at my old job.
My English is getting better every week.

exercises.hintUz: oʻzgarish (holatga oʻtish) + ogʻzaki nutq → got + sifat

exercises.prompt: With new beliefs, he ___ a confident person.
exercises.hintUz: become + ot; “to” qoʻshilmaydi va shakli became

exercises.prompt: The sky ___ grey before the storm.
exercises.hintUz: rang oʻzgarishi uchun → turn + sifat: turned grey

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting: bir yil oldin qanday edingiz va endi qanday oʻzgaryapsiz — “A year ago I was … , but I am becoming … / my … is getting … ”.
exercises.hintUz: get/become + sifat; oʻtmish uchun was, oʻzgarish uchun is getting/am becoming

vocab.defEn: the collection of global beliefs about who you are
vocab.example: You can choose and change your identity.

vocab.uz: global (tub) eʼtiqod — hamma narsaga taʼsir qiladigan ishonch
vocab.defEn: a fundamental belief that affects everything else
vocab.example: Global beliefs shape all your other beliefs.

vocab.defEn: a negative belief that holds you back
vocab.example: "I'm not a good language learner" is a limiting belief.

vocab.defEn: a positive belief that gives you strength

vocab.uz: xotira ichiga kirmoq, uni toʻliq his qilmoq
vocab.defEn: to step into an experience and feel it fully
vocab.example: Associate with your positive memories to make them stronger.

vocab.defEn: to disconnect yourself from an experience
vocab.example: You can disassociate from negative memories to weaken them.

vocab.defEn: a specific example that supports a belief
vocab.example: The more references you find, the stronger the belief.

vocab.defEn: to interpret an experience through your beliefs first
vocab.example: We filter every experience through our beliefs.

vocab.defEn: to be unable to escape or change something
vocab.example: You are not stuck with the identity you have now.

vocab.defEn: to develop and strengthen something over time
vocab.example: You can build up a positive identity step by step.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Ben ish qidirishga kirishadi va oʻzini boʻlajak muvaffaqiyatli inson deb tasavvur qiladi — garchi u atigi oʻn besh yoshda boʻlsa ham. Bu darsning “identity” gʻoyasiga mos: kim boʻlishni tanlash. Sizningcha, Ben oʻzi haqidagi kuchli eʼtiqodi bilan ishga ega boʻladimi yoki bu bolalarcha xayolmi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Ben starts job hunting and pictures himself as a future success — even though he's only fifteen. It fits the identity theme: choosing who you want to become. Do you think Ben's strong self-belief will get him the job, or is it just a boyish dream?

keyVocab.defEn: actively looking to buy or get something
a database entry advertising an available job

keyVocab.uz: rezyume (taʼlim va ish tajribasi qisqacha bayoni)
keyVocab.defEn: a short summary of your academic and work history

keyVocab.uz: xatni aniq bir kishiga yoʻllamoq/nomlab yozmoq
keyVocab.defEn: to direct a letter or statement to a specific person
very poor; almost totally without money

sixmin.title: Who do you think you are?

quiz.q: What percentage of the UK population described themselves as ethnically mixed?
quiz.qUz: Buyuk Britaniya aholisining necha foizi oʻzini aralash etnik kelib chiqishga mansub deb bilgan?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: a) 0.9%. Neil aynan 0.9% dedi va topdi (“you were on the money”). BBC 2011-yilgi soʻrovga koʻra aholining 0.9% oʻzini aralash irqli deb bilgan, garchi haqiqiy raqam 2% yoki undan koʻproq deb taxmin qilinadi.

vocab.uz: oʻzlik; inson kim yoki nima ekani
vocab.defEn: who or what a person is
relating to race or ethnic origin

vocab.uz: turli xalqlar qorishib yangi madaniyat yaratadigan jamiyat
vocab.defEn: a society made of people from different countries who create a new shared culture
to become part of a group, often changing your habits and customs to fit in
important for the development of something

vocab.en: to wear your heart on your sleeve
vocab.defEn: to make your feelings clear and open to everyone

funEnglish.title: Who am I? A philosophical inquiry - Amy Adkins

speakingPrompt.en: Tell the story of how you have changed. What kind of person were you a few years ago? What happened, and who did you become? How is your English getting better now?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: oʻtgan zamon hikoyasi va ketma-ketlik (I was… First… Then… Finally…) hamda oʻzgarishni ifodalash (I became…, I got…, my English is getting better).
